message:
Implement complete SearchClient with query execution and filtering

Replaced stub execute() function with a full-featured SearchClient that
provides read-only search operations against the Tantivy full-text index.

Architecture:
- SearchClient wraps IndexReader (not IndexWriter) for concurrent reads
- Constructed via open() which returns Option<Self> if index exists
- Holds Fields reference for type-safe document field access
- Searcher is ephemeral, created per-query from the long-lived reader

Query execution flow:
1. Parse user query string with QueryParser for title+content fields
2. Build BooleanQuery combining:
   - Must clause: parsed text query (if non-empty)
   - Must clause: agent filter with Should terms (OR logic across agents)
3. Execute search with TopDocs collector respecting limit
4. Extract and denormalize results into SearchHit structs

SearchFilters changes:
- Changed agents from Vec<String> to HashSet<String> for deduplication
- Agent filter translates to TermQuery instances combined with Should
  (matching any of the specified agent slugs)

SearchHit struct:
- Renamed from SearchResult for clarity (SearchResult often implies
  metadata wrapper, SearchHit emphasizes the concrete document match)
- Fields: title, snippet (first line of content), score, source_path
- Snippet extraction uses lines().next() for simple preview text

Helper function:
- open_search_client(&TantivyIndex, &Path) provides ergonomic construction
- Takes index reference to extract Fields, uses Path for Index::open_in_dir
- Returns Ok(None) if index doesn't exist yet (graceful degradation)

This implementation enables the TUI to perform real searches with agent
filtering. Future enhancements can add workspace, time range, and other
filters by extending SearchFilters and adding corresponding clauses to
the BooleanQuery construction.

Dependencies: Requires public Fields from tantivy.rs (previous commit).
